behaviors.description: The evaluate-stage command stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact.
behaviors.justification: Slot A (evidence): "evaluate-stage command stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact." Slot B (boundary): stage command wiring to artifact persistence slot selection (`grounding` vs `plan`). Slot C (lower-level miss-proof): a Unit test of a mapper/helper can pass while command-to-store integration still writes the wrong artifact key. Rejection gate: rejected System because behavior text is only about artifact slot routing, not OS/network/subprocess; rejected Agentic/Workflow because it does not require judging model output or multiple agentic decisions. Final level is Integration based on Slot B and Slot C.
behaviors.test_description: Run the evaluate command through its real stage entrypoint with an in-process artifact store and run context; assert a new `grounding` artifact is written and `plan` is untouched.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test exercises module handoff (stage -> artifact store), which is exactly the chosen Integration boundary.

behaviors.description: The implement-stage flow reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`.
behaviors.justification: Slot A (evidence): "implement-stage flow reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`." Slot B (boundary): implement orchestration must read one artifact and write another across stage/store interfaces. Slot C (lower-level miss-proof): Unit tests of read/write helpers can miss regression where composed flow reads grounding but accidentally overwrites wrong output slot. Rejection gate: rejected Agentic because behavior text targets routing/orchestration, not quality of model content; rejected Workflow because this behavior can be validated within one stage execution plus pre-seeded artifact state; rejected System due no real OS/network requirement in the behavior text. Final level is Integration based on Slot B and Slot C.
behaviors.test_description: Seed `grounding` artifact in the same run context, execute implement flow with deterministic model stub, assert grounding is consumed by flow context and resulting execution output is persisted to `plan` only.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Assertions depend on multi-module stage/store interaction, not just local function logic.

behaviors.description: Deep-dive no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect when diagnosis already exists in issue content.
behaviors.justification: Slot A (evidence): "Deep-dive no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect when diagnosis already exists in issue content." Slot B (boundary): deep-dive stage decision logic plus artifact-write side effect boundary. Slot C (lower-level miss-proof): Unit logic test for "diagnosis exists" can pass while actual stage still emits unintended write through integration path. Rejection gate: rejected System because the failure is side-effect routing in local stage/store wiring; rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior is about suppressing artifact overwrite, not validating model judgment across multiple agentic steps. Final level is Integration based on Slot B and Slot C.
behaviors.test_description: Execute deep-dive stage with issue content containing diagnosis and a pre-existing `plan`; verify stage completion does not create/update `plan` artifact.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires observing real side effects across stage + artifact writer modules.

behaviors.description: Artifact lineage is preserved so implement can read the `grounding` artifact produced earlier in the same run context.
behaviors.justification: Slot A (evidence): "Artifact lineage is preserved so implement can read the `grounding` artifact produced earlier in the same run context." Slot B (boundary): cross-stage lineage metadata and lookup across run-context/artifact modules. Slot C (lower-level miss-proof): Unit tests of lineage ID helpers can pass while end-to-end lookup in implement fails due wiring/key/version mismatch. Rejection gate: rejected Workflow because behavior text requires lineage handoff correctness, not correctness of multiple model decisions; rejected Agentic because model output quality is not the oracle; rejected System because no real OS/network/subprocess is required by text. Final level is Integration based on Slot B and Slot C.
behaviors.test_description: Run evaluate to produce `grounding`, then run implement in the same run context using real lineage lookup path; assert implement resolves that exact grounding artifact by lineage/reference metadata.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: This directly tests cross-module handoff and retrieval, matching Integration.

behaviors.description: In a full multi-step agent run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`), the final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier in the run.
behaviors.justification: Slot A (evidence): "In a full multi-step agent run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`), the final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier in the run." Slot B (boundary): sequential handoff across multiple agentic stages where earlier stage output must influence later stage plan content. Slot C (lower-level miss-proof): Integration/Agentic single-step tests can pass while full chain loses grounding influence during intermediate handoff or replan transitions. Rejection gate: rejected System as insufficient because behavior requires validating multi-step agent pipeline semantics, not just infra I/O. Final level is Workflow based on Slot B and Slot C.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a real multi-step run with actual stage chain (`deep-dive -> evaluate -> implement`) and assert final `plan` includes/reflects grounding facts generated earlier in-run.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test explicitly requires multiple sequential agentic steps, which matches Workflow.

behaviors.description: When grounding is absent, implement falls back safely and still emits a valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts.
behaviors.justification: Slot A (evidence): "When grounding is absent, implement falls back safely and still emits a valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts." Slot B (boundary): fallback branch plus artifact write isolation across implement/store modules. Slot C (lower-level miss-proof): Unit fallback logic test can pass while real flow still mutates unrelated artifacts due repository transaction/wiring behavior. Rejection gate: rejected System because behavior text is local artifact consistency, not OS/network/subprocess; rejected Agentic because correctness criterion is artifact integrity and fallback control flow, not model output quality; rejected Workflow because this can be validated in one implement invocation with prepared artifact state. Final level is Integration based on Slot B and Slot C.
behaviors.test_description: Prepare run context with non-grounding artifacts only, execute implement flow, assert a valid `plan` artifact is created and all pre-existing non-target artifacts remain unchanged.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires module interaction (fallback + persistence isolation), which is above Unit and below System/Agentic.
